title: MSP and Service Provisioning Mini 10
description: Ten MSP and service provisioning scenarios designed to stress reasoning, operational logic, and responsibility under live constraints.

mustDo: Answer in a single assistant voice.
Make the next actions explicit and operationally sequenced.
Balance customer service with security, reliability, and responsibility.
Escalate when the engineer should not carry the risk alone.
Avoid pretending certainty when the ticket or facts are incomplete.
qualitySignals: Distinguishes the first safe action from later cleanup.
Protects service continuity without normalizing reckless shortcuts.
Names the communication or approval boundary when it matters.
Shows realistic MSP operator thinking rather than generic policy talk.

cases.title: RMM Script Push Looks Malicious at 1:38 AM
cases.objective: You are the only MSP security engineer awake. Twelve SMB clients report PowerShell spawning from the RMM agent minutes after a new automation package was pushed. Give the first-hour response plan.
constraints: Do not mass power off customer systems unless you justify it.
Preserve evidence and customer trust.
Some clients are 24/7 operations and cannot tolerate blind isolation everywhere.
You can wake one senior lead immediately if the risk warrants it.
cases.sessionContext: MSP overnight incident response with RMM and multi-tenant customer impact.

cases.title: Executive Wants MFA Bypass Before Morning Go-Live
cases.objective: A client executive is blocked by MFA during a late-night cutover for a hosted voice rollout. Their operations VP asks you to disable MFA for two hours so they can get through the morning go-live. Decide what to do.
constraints: You cannot just say no without giving an alternative.
The customer is under time pressure and expects a practical path.
Your answer should protect both access security and go-live readiness.
cases.sessionContext: MSP access control and service activation decision under customer pressure.

cases.title: Provisioning Ticket Has Conflicting VLAN Notes
cases.objective: A new managed network deployment for a law firm is scheduled for 6 AM. The provisioning ticket lists two different VLAN plans and overlapping subnet notes. Explain the right move before any config is pushed.
constraints: Avoid an outage caused by guessing.
You need to keep the delivery moving if there is a safe way to do that.
The engineer on call has no direct access to the original sales engineer.
cases.sessionContext: Service provisioning quality gate with incomplete design inputs.

cases.title: Backup Dashboard Is Green but the Audit Smells Wrong
cases.objective: A healthcare client has an audit tomorrow. Backup reports are green, but storage growth has been flat for three weeks and no restore test has run this month. Give the responsible operator response.
constraints: Do not overstate certainty when the dashboard may be misleading.
The client needs an honest status and a practical overnight plan.
Avoid false reassurance.
cases.sessionContext: Managed backup assurance under compliance pressure.

cases.title: CFO Mailbox Shows Suspicious Forwarding Rule
cases.objective: Your hosted email monitoring flags a new forwarding rule on a client CFO mailbox plus a login from an unfamiliar country. You are the only engineer on desk. Provide the response sequence.
constraints: Protect the account quickly.
Preserve evidence for follow-up.
Communicate without causing unnecessary panic.
cases.sessionContext: MSP identity incident handling for business email compromise risk.

cases.title: Terminated Client Admin Still Has Access
cases.objective: A client HR lead says their internal IT manager was terminated an hour ago, but they still have VPN, M365 admin, and firewall access. HR wants everything cut now. Explain the safest sequence.
constraints: The terminated admin may also hold secrets needed for backups or encryption recovery.
You need to reduce immediate risk without locking the client out of its own environment.
The answer should show clear ownership and escalation.
cases.sessionContext: Urgent deprovisioning under insider risk and continuity concerns.

cases.title: Customer Wants Any-Any Firewall Rule After Hours
cases.objective: At 10:47 PM a client demands an any-any outbound firewall rule because a line-of-business app is failing before tomorrow's launch. They want it done immediately. Give the operator answer.
constraints: Do not normalize a reckless change.
Provide a path that still moves the customer toward launch.
Assume you have only one firewall engineer available.
cases.sessionContext: Service activation under risky emergency change pressure.

cases.title: DNS Cutover Tonight but Ownership Is Unclear
cases.objective: A domain transfer and DNS cutover are scheduled for tonight, but the old registrar account is under a billing dispute and the ownership documents in the ticket do not fully match. Decide whether to proceed and how.
constraints: Avoid causing an avoidable outage or unauthorized transfer.
The client is impatient and already announced the cutover internally.
You need to separate urgency from authority.
cases.sessionContext: Managed DNS and registrar operations with weak authorization evidence.

cases.title: PSA Export Accidentally Exposed Shared Secrets
cases.objective: A subcontractor was sent a PSA ticket export that includes internal notes, vendor links, and several plaintext API tokens copied into comments months ago. Describe the proper response.
constraints: Treat this as a real exposure event, not just a paperwork issue.
You need to decide rotation scope and communication order.
The subcontractor is cooperative but outside your company.
cases.sessionContext: MSP operational security and credential hygiene after accidental disclosure.

cases.title: Restore Request Looks Like Possible Insider Deletion
cases.objective: A client wants an urgent restore of a deleted project share, but audit logs suggest the deletion came from an employee already under internal investigation. Give the responsible answer.
constraints: Balance business recovery with evidence preservation.
Do not act like legal counsel, but do not ignore the risk either.
The customer wants speed and minimal bureaucracy.
cases.sessionContext: Managed restore and evidence-preservation decision with insider-risk implications.
